\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\caption{fPINN forward-problem convergence study on the manufactured solution $y_{\mathrm{ext}}$ of Lemma~\ref{lem:sharpness}. For each $\alpha$, the network is trained on the $\mathcal{I}_h$-restricted residual loss of \eqref{eq:fpinn_loss} \textup{(}with $\lambda_{D}=\lambda_{0}=0$\textup{)} on a uniform mesh of $[0,T_{\max}]$ for several $h$ and 5 seeds. Reported quantities at $\gamma = \alpha + 2.0$: $\hat p_{\mathrm{err}}$, the slope of the log--log regression of the mean error $\|y_{\mathrm{net}}-y_{\mathrm{ext}}\|_\infty$ versus $h$; $\hat p_{\mathrm{res}}$, the analogous slope for the full-mesh L1 residual $\|\mathcal{L}_h y_{\mathrm{net}}-a\|_{\infty,\{0,\dots,N-1\}}$ \textup{(}stricter than the $\mathcal{I}_h$-restricted loss used during training, reported as a diagnostic\textup{)}; and $2-\alpha$, the worst-case theoretical envelope of Theorem~\ref{thm:L1_consistency}. The empirical error rates exceed the worst-case envelope across the full sweep, consistent with the variational consistency estimate of Theorem~\ref{thm:fpinn_consistency} and illustrating the interior cancellation mechanism discussed below Theorem~\ref{thm:L1_consistency}.}
\label{tab:fpinn_forward}
